answers.answer: B) Run the container interactively with `docker run -it <image> /bin/sh` to inspect the environment and run the entrypoint manually. Reason: When a container exits immediately with no logs, running interactively lets you: (1) see the actual environment; (2) manually run the entrypoint command to see errors; (3) check file permissions, missing files, environment variables. Option A (no-cache) won't help debug. Option C (memory) would show OOM errors. Option D (daemon restart) is unlikely to be the issue.

JQ COMMANDS FOR EVENT ANALYSIS:

1. Count events by type (sorted desc):
```bash
jq 'group_by(.event) | map({event: .[0].event, count: length}) | sort_by(-.count)' events.json
```

2. Top 10 users by total session duration:
```bash
jq 'group_by(.user_id) | map({user_id: .[0].user_id, total_duration: map(.properties.duration_ms // 0) | add}) | sort_by(-.total_duration) | .[0:10]' events.json
```

3. Average page view duration per page (mobile only):
```bash
jq '[.[] | select(.properties.device == "mobile" and .event == "page_view")] | group_by(.properties.page) | map({page: .[0].properties.page, avg_duration: (map(.properties.duration_ms) | add / length)})' events.json
```

4. Sessions with >5 page views:
```bash
jq '[.[] | select(.event == "page_view")] | group_by(.session_id) | map({session_id: .[0].session_id, page_count: length}) | map(select(.page_count > 5))' events.json
```

5. Funnel analysis (/products -> /cart -> /checkout):
```bash
jq '
  group_by(.user_id) |
  map({
    user_id: .[0].user_id,
    viewed_products: (map(.properties.page) | any(. == "/products")),
    viewed_cart: (map(.properties.page) | any(. == "/cart")),
    viewed_checkout: (map(.properties.page) | any(. == "/checkout"))
  }) |
  {
    products_viewed: map(select(.viewed_products)) | length,
    cart_viewed: map(select(.viewed_products and .viewed_cart)) | length,
    checkout_viewed: map(select(.viewed_products and .viewed_cart and .viewed_checkout)) | length,
    products_to_cart_pct: ((map(select(.viewed_products and .viewed_cart)) | length) / (map(select(.viewed_products)) | length) * 100),
    cart_to_checkout_pct: ((map(select(.viewed_products and .viewed_cart and .viewed_checkout)) | length) / (map(select(.viewed_products and .viewed_cart)) | length) * 100)
  }
' events.json
```

6. Busiest hour of day:
```bash
jq 'map(.timestamp | split("T")[1] | split(":")[0]) | group_by(.) | map({hour: .[0], count: length}) | sort_by(-.count) | .[0]' events.json
```

7. Detect bot traffic (>100 events in 1-minute window):
```bash
jq '
  group_by(.user_id) |
  map({
    user_id: .[0].user_id,
    events: .,
    max_per_minute: (
      group_by(.timestamp | split(":")[0:2] | join(":")) |
      map(length) |
      max
    )
  }) |
  map(select(.max_per_minute > 100))
' events.json
```

8. CSV report (user_id, total_events, unique_pages, avg_duration, device_breakdown):
```bash
jq -r '
  group_by(.user_id) |
  map({
    user_id: .[0].user_id,
    total_events: length,
    unique_pages: ([.[].properties.page] | unique | length),
    avg_duration: (map(.properties.duration_ms // 0) | add / length),
    device_breakdown: (group_by(.properties.device) | map("\(.[0].properties.device):\(length)") | join("; "))
  }) |
  ["user_id,total_events,unique_pages,avg_duration,device_breakdown"],
  (.[] | [.user_id, .total_events, .unique_pages, (.avg_duration | tostring), .device_breakdown] | join(","))
' events.json
```
